2026-04-23T05:22:42.2878670Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-23T05:22:42.2879215Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-23T05:22:42.2879632Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-23T05:22:42.2879985Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-23T05:22:42.2880285Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T05:22:42.2880511Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T05:22:42.2880661Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-23T05:22:42.2880831Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T05:22:42.2880992Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-23T05:22:42.2881213Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-23T05:22:42.2881676Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-23T05:22:42.2881923Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

section is empty** — the heading exists under \\u0026#34;Getting started\\u0026#34; but has no content; provisioning libraries should explain how to set up credentials (e.g., `DefaultAzureCredential` with `AzureDeveloperCliCredential` via the `azd` deployment workflow)\\n- **\\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section lacks resource descriptions** — it contains one generic sentence about declarative provisioning but does not explain what the four resource types do (TrafficController/Application Gateway for Containers, Frontend, Association, SecurityPolicy)\\n- **`ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` is undocumented in Examples** — the CHANGELOG lists it as a key feature but there is no example in the README\\n- **\\u0026#34;Next steps\\u0026#34; section is empty** — the heading exists but has no content (links to AGfC documentation, tutorials, or related provisioning packages are missing)\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File to edit:** `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/README.md`\\n\\nFill in the four gaps in the existing README. Do NOT restructure the file — only add content to the sections that are currently empty or thin.\\n\\n**1. \\u0026#34;Authenticate the Client\\u0026#34; section** — add after the `### Authenticate the Client` heading:\\n\\n```markdown\\nNo explicit authentication is needed in this library. `Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking`\\ngenerates Bicep/ARM templates that are deployed by [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\nAuthentication to Azure is handled by `azd` at deployment time, typically via\\n[`DefaultAzureCredential`]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n```\\n\\n**2. \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section** — replace or augment the single existing sentence with:\\n\\n```markdown\\nThis library allows you to specify your infrastructure in a declarative style using .NET. You can then use `azd` to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain Bicep or ARM templates.\\n\\n### Resource types\\n\\n| Type | Description |\\n|------|-------------|\\n| `TrafficController` | Represents an [Application Gateway for Containers]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) resource — the top-level traffic controller. |\\n| `TrafficControllerFrontend` | A frontend endpoint on a `TrafficController` that receives inbound traffic. |\\n| `TrafficControllerAssociation` | Associates a subnet with a `TrafficController` so it can route traffic. |\\n| `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` | A security policy resource that can be attached to a `TrafficController` to enforce WAF or other security rules. |\\n```\\n\\n**3. \\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34; section** — add a second example after the existing \\u0026#34;Create a Traffic Controller\\u0026#34; snippet:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Create a Security Policy\\n\\nThis example demonstrates how to create an `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` and attach it to an existing `TrafficController`.\\n\\n```\\nInfrastructure infra = new();\\n\\nTrafficController controller =\\n    new(nameof(controller), TrafficController.ResourceVersions.V2025_01_01);\\ninfra.Add(controller);\\n\\nApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy policy =\\n    new(nameof(policy), ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy.ResourceVersions.V2025_01_01)\\n    {\\n        Parent = controller,\\n    };\\ninfra.Add(policy);\\n` `` `\\n```\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** add the `#region Snippet:ServiceNetworkingSecurityPolicy` / `#endregion` markers around the corresponding code in the test file (see Step 2 below) before running `Update-Snippets.ps1`.\\n\\n**4. \\u0026#34;Next steps\\u0026#34; section is empty** — the heading exists under \\u0026#34;Getting started\\u0026#34; but has no content; provisioning libraries should explain how to set up credentials (e.g., `DefaultAzureCredential` with `AzureDeveloperCliCredential` via the `azd` deployment workflow)\\n- **\\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section lacks resource descriptions** — it contains one generic sentence about declarative provisioning but does not explain what the four resource types do (TrafficController/Application Gateway for Containers, Frontend, Association, SecurityPolicy)\\n- **`ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` is undocumented in Examples** — the CHANGELOG lists it as a key feature but there is no example in the README\\n- **\\u0026#34;Next steps\\u0026#34; section is empty** — the heading exists but has no content (links to AGfC documentation, tutorials, or related provisioning packages are missing)\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File to edit:** `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/README.md`\\n\\nFill in the four gaps in the existing README. You can then use `azd` to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain Bicep or ARM templates.\\n\\n### Resource types\\n\\n| Type | Description |\\n|------|-------------|\\n| `TrafficController` | Represents an [Application Gateway for Containers]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) resource — the top-level traffic controller. |\\n| `TrafficControllerFrontend` | A frontend endpoint on a `TrafficController` that receives inbound traffic. |\\n| `TrafficControllerAssociation` | Associates a subnet with a `TrafficController` so it can route traffic. |\\n| `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` | A security policy resource that can be attached to a `TrafficController` to enforce WAF or other security rules. |\\n```\\n\\n**3. \\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34; section** — add a second example after the existing \\u0026#34;Create a Traffic Controller\\u0026#34; snippet:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Create a Security Policy\\n\\nThis example demonstrates how to create an `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` and attach it to an existing `TrafficController`.\\n\\n```\\nInfrastructure infra = new();\\n\\nTrafficController controller =\\n    new(nameof(controller), TrafficController.ResourceVersions.V2025_01_01);\\ninfra.Add(controller);\\n\\nApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy policy =\\n    new(nameof(policy), ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy.ResourceVersions.V2025_01_01)\\n    {\\n        Parent = controller,\\n    };\\ninfra.Add(policy);\\n` `` `\\n```\\n\\n\\u0026gt;
